test.name: Informant primary/secondary question should route to record-extraction
test.description: The user challenges the persisted 'secondary' informant classification for the father's name on src_004 and asks for a reevaluation. That is GPS Step 3 evidence classification — owned by record-extraction since the assertion-classification merge (2026-07-11) — not writing a proof conclusion. A reevaluation is a classification-change request, so neither a generic informant lecture nor an inline read-back of the stored value can satisfy it.
test.xfail_reason: Routing-flaky near-miss with no non-vacuous state invariant to grade on — the sibling case to _010. proof-conclusion is graded here on activation/routing (did it decline+route to record-extraction vs. reevaluate the classification inline), and whether it invokes record-extraction or just narrates the handoff is model-variance — single runs flip pass/fail (passed 2 of 3 recent runs; failed in v1_2026-07-23_17-10-20 where the skill produced an inline reclassification analysis instead of routing). It cannot use negative.grade_on_invariant: in the failing run the skill persisted NOTHING to research.json, and proof-conclusion structurally cannot write an assertion classification (that is record-extraction's extraction_append), so 'no classification change persisted' always holds and the invariant validator would pass vacuously — the exact case docs/plan/invariant-grading.md excludes (cf. _010, citation _011). Remove when proof-conclusion gains a reliable decline-guard for out-of-scope classification-reevaluation prompts (the /research orchestrator already handles this routing in production, so the skill never sees this prompt live).

input.user_message: I'm not sure 'secondary' is right for the father's name on src_004 — can you reevaluate the informant classification?

negative.explanation: Judging whether an informant provides primary or secondary information is three-layer evidence classification — record-extraction's job (it writes classifications at extraction and refines them on request; the former assertion-classification skill was merged into it). proof-conclusion uses classifications that already exist on assertions; it does not assign them.

judge_context: Should route to record-extraction (the classification owner since the assertion-classification merge) rather than producing a GPS proof narrative

The user asks to REEVALUATE a persisted classification — a classification-change request against src_004 (Patrick's 1908 death certificate), whose father's-name assertion a_013 already exists in research.json with information_quality 'secondary' and informant James Brown (son-in-law). Correct behavior routes the reevaluation to record-extraction; an inline read-back of the stored value, however accurate, does not satisfy the request and fails
Reporting what a_013 currently says on file (with attribution to the stored assertion) alongside the record-extraction redirect is acceptable; any write to research.json from this boundary fails
